mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6.0%, labor market slack signals deteriorating consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive in >55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 6.0%, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and capital demand expansion, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce a positive Sharpe ratio > 0.5 over 60-day horizons, or RSAFS and HOUST show divergent trends without corresponding equity outperformance.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during periods of stable inflation reflects healthy capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, pushing S&P 500 returns positive over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases coincide with negative 20-day SPX returns when CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth exceeds 0.4%, invalidating the supply-side momentum premise.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month gains in total nonfarm payrolls signal strengthening economic activity and wage growth, which translates to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS increases fail to predict positive 5-day forward returns (Rank IC < 0.01) across multiple rolling windows, or the signal reverses direction during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 255.0, elevated inflation expectations force monetary tightening fears, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 20-day returns remain positive in >60% of observations where CPIAUCSL exceeds 255.0, or the inflation threshold loses predictive power (p > 0.05) in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly growth in real GDP and monthly retail sales confirms broad-based demand expansion, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined GDPC1 and RSAFS growth signal yields negative 60-day forward returns in >50% of cases, or the interaction term shows no incremental predictive value over single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production alongside rising unemployment indicates a supply-demand mismatch and economic contraction, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict negative 5-day returns when INDPRO and UNRATE move in opposite directions, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 across rolling 2-year windows.
